reflection_summary: Agent still hovers and never lands despite previous attempts to weaken r_approach_region and strengthen terminal bonuses. The last structural search (r_landing_region_progress with weight 2.0) collapsed the reward signal. A new structural search is needed to add a low-weight descent bridging reward that connects hovering to landing without overshadowing other components.

observed_facts: Shaping/goal mismatch flag true, repeated_event_exploitation due to bouncing
Previous edits (decrease approach weight + relax duration, decrease approach weight + increase terminal weight, add landing_region_progress with weight 2.0) all failed to produce any terminal payment
likely_true_failures: Agent fails to land; task not solved
Policy stuck in local optimum of hovering/bouncing near pad
No intermediate gradient from hovering to terminal landing exists
likely_false_positives: repeated_event_exploitation is a symptom of bouncing, not deliberate hacking
high_reward_low_progress flag false; component_dominance raw flag false
root_cause_hypotheses: Current shaping rewards do not provide a gradient toward the precise landing event; the agent is content to maximize r_approach_region and other dense scores without ever triggering the terminal condition
Previous bridging attempt with high weight (2.0) collapsed the reward landscape; a much gentler bridging signal (weight ≤0.5) is needed

reusable_lessons: Coupled rebalancing (shaping+terminal) can shift focus but must be strong enough
One-time event bonuses protect against exploitation if they are triggerable
Structural search with a new dense component must use very low weight to avoid dominating the signal
failed_or_weak_lessons: iter_000_to_001 (decrease r_approach_region + relax duration) too weak; terminal never paid
iter_002_to_003 (decrease r_approach_region + increase terminal weight) caused slight degradation, no landing
iter_004_to_005 (add r_landing_region_progress with weight 2.0) caused massive reward collapse; confirmed that high weight on a new shaping component is dangerous
avoid_actions: Solo decrease of dominant shaping component without a strong terminal boost
Increasing terminal bonus weight without verifying the event is reachable
Adding any new dense shaping component with weight >0.5
Repeating the exact same edit combinations as iter_000_to_001 or iter_002_to_003 or iter_004_to_005
recommended_actions: Structural search for a low-weight (≤0.5) bridging reward component that encourages descent (e.g., negative altitude, vertical speed toward pad)
Optionally pair with a mild further decrease of r_approach_region (factor ~0.7) only if the new bridging component is proven to be present
Maintain all one-time terminal flags and current constraints on energy penalty

strategy.why_atomic_or_separable: Adding a new low-weight component is a structural change that can be applied alone; a mild decrease of r_approach_region can be added as a separate step after evaluating the new component’s effect if needed. They do not require atomic coupling.
editor_constraints: Any new component must have weight ≤ 0.5 to avoid becoming a new dominant dense reward
New component should be a simple descent indicator: e.g., metric_delta on negative altitude or vertical speed with positive_only=true
If using a metric_delta, ensure the metric is available (e.g., 'altitude', 'vertical_speed')
Do not remove r_approach_region entirely; if decreasing it, use factor ≥ 0.7
Maintain one-time flags on all terminal bonuses
Total expected episode reward should stay within [1, 500]
Do not increase r_energy penalty beyond -0.05
must_preserve: r_approach_progress (delta positive only) for directional signal
r_safe_contact_once and r_stable_landing_once as one-time terminal bonuses
must_avoid: Adding dense shaping components that overshadow the terminal goal (e.g., weight >0.5 on a progress reward)
Reducing all shaping components to near zero without a reliable terminal gradient
strategy.expected_effect: After retraining, the agent should begin to descend toward the pad instead of hovering. Initial mean reward may drop, but eventually terminal event will become reachable, leading to first stable landing and a rise in success rate.
strategy.risk_analysis: If the new bridging reward is still insufficient (weight too low or metric not informative), the terminal event may remain unreachable, and reward could drop further. If the new component is too high, it may cause the agent to bounce without stable landing. Monitoring after retraining is essential; if no terminal payment after 2 iterations, consider further relaxing terminal condition definition or exploring different descent metrics.

package_should_be_rejected_if: r_approach_region weight is reduced to zero
Terminal bonus one-time flag is removed
Total expected episode reward exceeds 500 or falls below 1
Multiple new complex components are added (keep it to one simple bridging reward)
package_should_be_accepted_if: It adds a moderate descent-based reward (weight ≤ 0.5) using negative altitude, vertical speed, or a similar simple metric
It optionally includes a small further decrease of r_approach_region (factor ≤ 0.7) to further weaken hovering
It preserves all one-time terminal bonuses and their conditions
It does not add multiple complex new components
safety_notes: Monitor contact_toggle_mean after retraining; a decrease indicates reduced bouncing
Check r_approach_progress trigger rate remains >0 to ensure approach signal persists

After retraining, if success_episode_rate remains 0, inspect whether stable_landing_condition is objectively achievable (e.g., requires legs contact + low speed + upright), and consider further relaxing via edit operators if possible
